summary: A Go testing and validation framework with MCP integration, using automated agents (GOrchestra) to systematically expand test coverage across internal packages.
patterns: MCP handler pattern (Model Context Protocol integration)
concerns: heavy reliance on automated agents (glm-agents) for test generation may produce low-quality or redundant tests
large number of agent directories (36+) suggests fragmented or incremental development without cohesive refactoring
potential tech debt from accumulated auto-generated test files without manual curation
internal package structure may be growing complex without clear module boundaries
suggestions: consolidate and review auto-generated tests for quality and relevance
consider extracting internal packages into separate modules if complexity grows
document the GOrchestra agent system and its conventions for maintainability
establish code review gates for agent-generated contributions
audit test coverage to ensure meaningful coverage rather than volume
